criteria_map: No active references remain: satisfied by the grep result over source, docs, deploy, and CI guard paths.

stress_test: A plausible failure mode was autonomous polling tests relying on the removed global default and accidentally executing real commands. The tests now inject a successful runner for execution-path assertions and keep explicit dry-run calls where command execution must be skipped.
C000.summary: P001 is solved. The active source contract no longer contains the global default switch, and tests prove both default execution and explicit dry-run behavior.

C003.summary: The root problem is solved. The transitional global dry-run path is gone from active source and docs, the cleaned controller is deployed and verified, explicit dry-run remains the only simulation path, stale untracked problem-package residue is removed, and remote branch residue has been deleted.

P000.description:
The release controller still contains a transitional global `dry_run_default` configuration path. That path makes runtime behavior depend on a hidden controller-wide switch instead of the explicit request contract. It also leaves misleading docs and examples that future humans or agents may copy.

The workspace also contains visible stale problem-package residue from earlier implementation phases. This cleanup must preserve useful committed history and current ledgers, but remove or quarantine files that are no longer part of the current product path.
success_criteria: `dry_run_default` no longer exists in release-controller source, sample config, tests, CI guards, deploy docs, or architecture docs.
Omitted `dry_run` means real execution for branch release, poll, promotion, and rollback planning; explicit `dry_run=true` remains the only dry-run behavior.
Tests prove omitted `dry_run` executes by default and explicit `dry_run=true` remains a simulation path.
Runtime config on the API host no longer contains `dry_run_default`.
A cleaned release-controller image is built, pushed, deployed, and verified healthy.
A trigger without `dry_run` performs a real staging release, while an explicit `dry_run=true` trigger does not update release pointers.
Stale local work-package/path residue is inventoried and only safe-to-remove residue is deleted or left with an explicit reason.
Relevant tests, CI guards, ledger validation/rendering, and runtime smoke checks pass.
P000.title: Remove release-controller transitional dry-run paths and stale work-package residue

P001.description: Active release-controller code, tests, sample config, and docs still expose `dry_run_default`. Remove this global switch so the only dry-run behavior is an explicit request field.
success_criteria: `ControllerConfig` has no `dry_run_default` field, parsing, validation, or serialized output.
`ReleasePlanner` resolves omitted `dry_run` to execution and explicit `dry_run=true` to simulation.
Sample config and docs describe the clean contract without global dry-run defaults.
Tests cover omitted `dry_run` executing by default and explicit dry-run still skipping command execution.
`rg dry_run_default` returns no matches in active release-controller/docs/deploy/CI paths after this child is complete.
P001.title: Remove dry_run_default from source contract

P002.description: After the source contract is cleaned, the API host must run the cleaned controller and runtime config must not preserve the removed `dry_run_default` key.
success_criteria: API-host `/opt/novaic/release-controller/config.json` has no `dry_run_default` key.
A release-controller image from the cleaned source is built, pushed, deployed, and reported healthy.
A trigger without `dry_run` performs real staging execution and records `dry_run=false`.
A trigger with explicit `dry_run=true` remains simulation-only and does not update release pointers.
Staging gateway, staging factory, and controller health checks pass after deployment.
P002.title: Deploy and verify cleaned release-controller runtime

P003.description: The working tree contains many stale problem-package paths and temporary files from earlier construction phases. Some may be useful history, some may be obsolete residue, and unrelated user changes must not be destroyed.
success_criteria: Stale local residue is inventoried with path groups and disposition.
Safe-to-delete residue is removed or archived only when it is not active product code and not needed for the current ledger.
Current ledger files are preserved.
Unrelated modified user files are left untouched unless explicitly identified as stale residue for this cleanup.
Final `git status --short` is explained, including any remaining unrelated dirty files.
P003.title: Clean stale workspace path residue safely

verification: `rg -n 'dry_run_default' novaic-release-controller docs/architecture/release-controller.md docs/runbooks/deploy.md deploy scripts/ci` returned no matches.

Verified staging published `753707c3f82dfcba175b63cf0bf7ced2a26f788c` and health checks pass.

The release-controller no longer has a global dry-run default path; omitted `dry_run` now means execute, explicit `dry_run=true` remains simulation. The cleaned controller is deployed on the API host, staging is running the cleaned commit, and stale untracked ledger residue plus old remote branches have been removed.

acceptance_criteria: Active release-controller source, config samples, docs, tests, deploy scripts, and CI guards contain no `dry_run_default` references.
Omitted `dry_run` resolves to real execution, while explicit `dry_run=true` remains simulation-only.
Runtime API-host config has no global dry-run default key.
A new cleaned release-controller image is deployed and healthy.
Runtime smoke tests prove default real staging release and explicit dry-run behavior.
Stale residue cleanup is performed only where safe, with an inventory of what was removed or intentionally left.
assumptions: `dry_run=true` is still a valid explicit operator simulation mode and is not considered transitional residue.
Production remains promotion/rollback guarded by existing branch rules and should not be branch-auto-deployed.
The user's approval covers deployment actions on the API host, but not blind deletion of unrelated unverified work.
T000.classification_reason: The cleanup spans active code contract changes, remote deployment verification, and stale residue deletion. Splitting keeps source edits, runtime migration, and path hygiene independently checkable.
T000.problem_definition: The release controller contains a misleading global `dry_run_default` branch. That branch contradicts the desired clean contract: requests execute unless they explicitly ask for `dry_run=true`. The workspace also has stale path residue from previous construction ledgers and temporary work packages that can mislead future maintenance.
T000.proposed_solution: Split the cleanup into bounded child problems. First remove the active `dry_run_default` code/config/docs/test path and pin the explicit dry-run contract with tests. Then deploy and verify the cleaned release-controller on the API host. Finally inventory stale local work-package/path residue and delete only items that are safe, unrelated to active product behavior, and supported by evidence.
risks: Automated branch polling is live, so source pushes may deploy staging while work is still in progress.
The workspace already has many unrelated dirty/untracked files; cleanup must not delete user work or unrelated historical material without evidence.
Removing the global default changes any caller that relied on omitted `dry_run` as simulation.
T000.title: Clean release-controller dry-run contract and stale residue
T000.verification_plan:
- Run targeted release-controller tests and release-controller CI guard tests.
- Run `rg dry_run_default` across active release-controller/docs/deploy/CI paths.
- Validate deploy script syntax and render/validate the problem ledger.
- Inspect remote runtime config, controller health, staging health, and release-controller trigger responses.
- Use `git status --short` to verify staged changes are scoped and unrelated user changes are preserved.

acceptance_criteria: No active source/config/docs/tests references to `dry_run_default` remain.
Omitted `dry_run` creates command plans with `dry_run=False`.
Explicit `dry_run=true` still creates dry-run command plans and skips command execution.
Release-controller tests and CI guard tests pass.
assumptions: Removing the global field is the intended non-conservative state, not an intermediate migration.
T001.classification_reason: The source cleanup is a bounded contract deletion with direct tests and grep verification; deployment and residue cleanup are separate child problems.
T001.problem_definition: The source contract still models a global `dry_run_default` even though the desired product behavior is explicit per-request dry-run only. This creates a misleading branch in model parsing, planner behavior, sample config, tests, and docs.
T001.proposed_solution: Remove the `dry_run_default` field and all parsing/serialization/docs references. Add or update planner and API tests so omitted `dry_run` resolves to `False` and explicit `dry_run=true` remains supported. Keep the `dry_run` request field and runner simulation behavior intact.
risks: Tests that used the old default as a convenience need explicit dry-run inputs instead of restoring a hidden default.
T001.title: Delete dry_run_default from active release-controller contract
T001.verification_plan:
- Run targeted release-controller tests.
- Run release-controller CI guard tests.
- Run `rg dry_run_default` over active release-controller/docs/deploy/CI paths.
- Inspect `git diff` for scoped cleanup without unrelated edits.

acceptance_criteria: Local cleaned source is committed and available to the API host build path.
API-host runtime config contains no removed global dry-run key.
New release-controller image digest is built, pushed, and deployed.
Controller `/health` passes and `/v1/status` reports polling healthy.
Manual trigger without `dry_run` records `execution.dry_run=false` and succeeds.
Manual trigger with `dry_run=true` records `execution.dry_run=true` and does not move the current release pointer.
Staging gateway and factory health checks pass after deployment.
assumptions: The API host has SSH/Docker/registry access as in the prior deployment work.
A real staging redeploy of the cleaned commit is acceptable.
T002.classification_reason: Runtime cleanup is a bounded deploy-and-smoke task now that source cleanup is closed; failures can be recorded or spawned as runtime blockers.
T002.problem_definition: The source contract is cleaned locally, but the API host may still carry the removed global key in runtime config and may still run an image built from the older code path.
T002.proposed_solution: Commit and push the cleaned source so the release-controller image can be built from immutable repository state. Remove the stale key from `/opt/novaic/release-controller/config.json`, build and push a new release-controller image on the API host from the cleaned commit, deploy it through `./deploy release-controller-image`, then verify controller health, staging health, real omitted-dry-run trigger behavior, and explicit dry-run behavior.
risks: Autonomous polling is live and may start a staging release after the commit is pushed.
Building images on the API host depends on remote Docker and registry availability.
Triggering the current commit twice may be skipped by operator expectations, but manual trigger should still produce a run.
T002.title: Deploy cleaned release-controller runtime
T002.verification_plan:
- Inspect remote config JSON with Python and verify the key is absent.
- Inspect running release-controller image digest and health endpoint.
- Use release-controller API trigger calls and compare release pointer before/after explicit dry-run.
- Curl local and public staging health endpoints.
- Run `git status --short` to verify only intended files are staged/committed.

acceptance_criteria: Current ledger `.complex-problems/L20260524-152814` remains intact.
Older untracked `.complex-problems` directories and loose old problem-package Markdown inputs are removed from the working tree.
Unrelated modified tracked files such as `docs-archive`, `scripts/start.sh`, and pre-existing dashboard deletion are not silently staged or reverted.
Final status clearly distinguishes remaining unrelated dirt from this cleanup's tracked changes.
assumptions: The active problem package for this work is `.complex-problems/L20260524-152814`.
Untracked old problem package bodies from prior dates are generated residue, not source-of-truth product code.
T003.classification_reason: The residue cleanup has a clear safe scope: remove old untracked problem-package files only, inventory branch/path residue, and leave unrelated tracked user changes untouched.
T003.problem_definition: The working tree contains stale paths from previous problem-package runs and other pre-existing dirty files. Some paths are likely generated residue, while others may be user work or historical documentation changes. The cleanup needs to reduce misleading clutter without destroying unrelated user changes.
T003.proposed_solution: Inventory dirty/untracked path groups, identify safe residue by ownership and current usefulness, delete only obsolete untracked problem-package files from older ledgers, preserve the current ledger, and leave unrelated modified/tracked files untouched with an explicit explanation.
risks: Old untracked ledger files might contain useful history, but they are not part of the current active ledger and have no committed owner.
Remote branch cleanup can be destructive if applied to unmerged branches; only safe evidence-based branch cleanup should happen in this ticket.
T003.title: Inventory and clean stale workspace residue
T003.verification_plan:
- Run `git status --short` and `find .complex-problems` before and after cleanup.
- Verify current ledger status with `ledger.py status` and `ledger.py validate` if available.
- Confirm `git status --short -- .complex-problems` only shows the current ledger.
- Inspect branch residue separately and delete only merged/safe branches if evidence supports it.
